channel.title: Serverless on OpenSwap Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Serverless on OpenSwap Guide

item.title: OpenFaaS vs Knative vs Apache OpenWhisk: Self-Hosted FaaS Guide 2026
item.description: Serverless computing doesn’t have to mean vendor lock-in. Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) platforms let you deploy event-driven code without managing servers, but public cloud offerings come with unpredictable billing, cold starts, and limited customization. Self-hosted FaaS platforms give you full control over your compute infrastructure while keeping the developer experience of writing and deploying individual functions.
